Operation.title: Operation
Operation.description: Represents a single execution or task performed by the Sovereign AI Platform, tracking its lifecycle, input parameters, and basic outcomes. This aligns with the requirement to 'save operations'.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Operation entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who initiated this operation. This ID is managed by an external authentication system. (Relationship: User 1:N Operation)

type.description: The category or type of operation (e.g., 'AI_Query', 'VulnerabilityScan', 'OsintTask', 'ForensicExtraction', 'MobileCrack').

name.description: A human-readable name or title for the operation instance.

endTime.description: Timestamp when the operation ended, if completed or failed.

inputParameters.description: JSON string of input parameters provided for the operation, allowing flexible data storage.

rawOutput.description: Raw output or logs generated by the operation. This could be large and may be stored as a file path or partial log.

reportIds.description: References to Reports generated from this operation. (Relationship: Operation 1:N Report)

Report.title: Report
Report.description: Represents a generated report, summarizing findings, operations, or knowledge. This aligns with the requirement to 'retrieve reports' and for the Smart Reporter.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Report entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who generated or owns this report. (Relationship: User 1:N Report)

type.description: The category or type of report (e.g., 'OSINT_Summary', 'Vulnerability_Assessment', 'AI_Conversation_Log', 'Forensic_Analysis', 'Attack_Automation_Summary').

generationTime.description: Timestamp when the report was generated.

content.description: The full content of the report, typically in markdown, HTML, or a structured JSON string format.

relatedOperationIds.description: References to Operations that contributed to this report. (Relationship: Report N:N Operation)

KnowledgeItem.title: KnowledgeItem
KnowledgeItem.description: Represents an individual piece of structured or unstructured knowledge ingested into the platform's Multi-KB RAG system. This supports the Learning & Knowledge Base features.

id.description: Unique identifier for the KnowledgeItem entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who generated or consumed this knowledge item. (Relationship: User 1:N KnowledgeItem)

content.description: The textual content of the knowledge item, which will be embedded for RAG.

source.description: The origin of the knowledge item (e.g., 'browser_history', 'chat_parser', 'forensic_extractor', 'user_input', 'deep_eye_scanner', 'osint_master', 'sliver_c2_log').

ingestedAt.description: Timestamp when the knowledge item was ingested into the system.

embeddingVectorId.description: Reference to the unique identifier of the vector in the ChromaDB for RAG retrieval.

relatedOperationId.description: Reference to the Operation that generated this knowledge item, if any. (Relationship: Operation 1:N KnowledgeItem)

AIModelConfiguration.title: AIModelConfiguration
AIModelConfiguration.description: Defines the configuration and capabilities of an AI model available to the Smart Router, including local Ollama models and external API models.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AIModelConfiguration entity.

name.description: The human-readable name of the AI model (e.g., 'qwen3:14b', 'mistral-large', 'KawaiiGPT').

modelIdentifier.description: The technical identifier used to invoke the model via its API or local Ollama instance.

capabilities.description: A list of capabilities or categories the model is best suited for (e.g., 'programming', 'cybersecurity', 'arabic_general', 'uncensored', 'redteam', 'blueteam').

description.description: A brief description of the model and its strengths.

apiKeyName.description: The environment variable name or configuration key for the API key, if applicable. (Actual API key is not stored).

endpointUrl.description: The API endpoint URL for external models or specific Ollama endpoints, if different from default.

isActive.description: Indicates if the model is currently active and available for use by the Smart Router.

ToolDefinition.title: ToolDefinition
ToolDefinition.description: Defines a tool or script available within the platform, used for dynamic menus in the Web Dashboard, Terminal, and Command Center. This allows central management of tool metadata.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ToolDefinition entity.

name.description: The display name of the tool (e.g., 'Deep Eye Scanner', 'OSINT Master', 'Extract APK').

description.description: A brief description of what the tool does.

category.description: The category the tool belongs to (e.g., 'OSINT', 'RedTeam', 'MobileForensics', 'AI_Engine', 'Utility', 'SocialBots').

scriptPath.description: The file system path to the executable script or entry point of the tool (e.g., 'security/deep_eye/deep_eye.py', 'mobile/advanced/extract_apk.sh').

usageSyntax.description: A template or example of how to invoke the tool, including expected arguments (e.g., 'python deep_eye.py --url <target>').

parametersSchema.description: JSON schema (as a string) describing the expected input parameters for the tool, enabling dynamic form generation in the UI.

isInteractive.description: Indicates if the tool requires interactive user input during execution (e.g., a TUI-based tool).

VulnerabilityScan.title: VulnerabilityScan
VulnerabilityScan.description: Represents a single web vulnerability scan performed by tools like Deep Eye, tracking its overall progress and collected findings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the VulnerabilityScan entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who initiated this scan. (Relationship: User 1:N VulnerabilityScan)

targetUrl.description: The base URL targeted by the scan.

scanType.description: The type of vulnerabilities scanned for (e.g., 'XSS', 'SQLi', 'CMDi', 'LFI', 'Comprehensive').

endTime.description: Timestamp when the scan ended, if completed.

findingIds.description: References to individual vulnerability findings discovered during this scan. (Relationship: VulnerabilityScan 1:N VulnerabilityFinding)

rawLog.description: Raw output or extensive logs generated by the scanner, potentially stored as a file path if very large.

VulnerabilityFinding.title: VulnerabilityFinding
VulnerabilityFinding.description: Represents a specific vulnerability discovered during a VulnerabilityScan, providing detailed information about the finding.

id.description: Unique identifier for the VulnerabilityFinding entity.

vulnerabilityScanId.description: Reference to the VulnerabilityScan that found this vulnerability. (Relationship: VulnerabilityScan 1:N VulnerabilityFinding)

type.description: The type of vulnerability (e.g., 'XSS', 'SQL Injection', 'LFI', 'Open Redirect', 'SSRF').

severity.description: The severity level of the vulnerability (e.g., 'Critical', 'High', 'Medium', 'Low', 'Informational').

description.description: A detailed description of the vulnerability, including its impact.

detectedUrl.description: The URL or specific endpoint where the vulnerability was detected.

payloadUsed.description: The payload or input that triggered the vulnerability, for reproduction.

remedialSuggestions.description: Suggestions for how to remediate or mitigate the vulnerability.

detectedAt.description: Timestamp when the vulnerability was detected.

OsintTask.title: OsintTask
OsintTask.description: Represents a single OSINT investigation task, tracking its parameters and overall outcome. This supports the OSINT Suite features.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OsintTask entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who initiated this OSINT task. (Relationship: User 1:N OsintTask)

target.description: The primary target of the OSINT investigation (e.g., a person's name, email address, phone number, domain, social media handle).

searchType.description: The type of OSINT search performed (e.g., 'PhoneLookup', 'SocialMediaScan', 'EmailBreachCheck', 'DomainAnalysis', 'FacialRecognition').

status.description: Current status of the OSINT task (e.g., 'pending', 'running', 'completed', 'failed').

startTime.description: Timestamp when the OSINT task started.

endTime.description: Timestamp when the OSINT task ended, if completed.

resultIds.description: References to individual OSINT results found by this task. (Relationship: OsintTask 1:N OsintResult)

rawLog.description: Raw output or extensive logs generated by the OSINT tools during this task, potentially stored as a file path.

OsintResult.title: OsintResult
OsintResult.description: Represents a specific finding or piece of information discovered during an OSINT task, providing structured data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OsintResult entity.

osintTaskId.description: Reference to the OsintTask that yielded this result. (Relationship: OsintTask 1:N OsintResult)

value.description: The primary value of the finding (e.g., '+1234567890', 'email@example.com', 'https://facebook.com/user').

description.description: A brief description or context of the finding.

sourceTool.description: The specific OSINT tool that discovered this result (e.g., 'facebook_osint', 'darkweb_scanner', 'image_analyzer').

sourceUrl.description: The URL or location where the information was found, if applicable.

discoveredAt.description: Timestamp when the result was discovered.

structuredData.description: Optional: JSON string for more complex, structured data associated with the finding (e.g., full EXIF metadata, parsed social media profile data).

definition.description: Stores individual operations initiated by a user. The 'userId' field within each document ensures authorization independence by matching the path wildcard.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who initiated the operation.

params.name: operationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific operation.

definition.description: Stores generated reports owned by a specific user. The 'userId' field within each document ensures authorization independence by matching the path wildcard.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who generated or owns this report.

params.name: reportId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific report.

definition.description: Stores individual pieces of knowledge for a user's RAG system. The 'userId' field within each document ensures authorization independence by matching the path wildcard.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who generated or consumed this knowledge item.

params.name: knowledgeItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific knowledge item.

definition.description: Stores global configurations for AI models available on the platform. Typically read-only for authenticated users, with write access restricted to administrators.

params.name: modelConfigId
params.description: The unique identifier for the AI model configuration.

definition.description: Stores definitions and metadata for various tools available on the platform. Accessible to all authenticated users for read.

params.name: toolDefinitionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the tool definition.

definition.description: Stores records of web vulnerability scans initiated by a specific user. The 'userId' field within each document ensures authorization independence by matching the path wildcard.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who initiated this scan.

params.name: scanId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific vulnerability scan.

definition.description: Stores individual vulnerability findings discovered within a user's scan. CRITICAL: This document must include a 'userId' field, denormalized from the owning user, for authorization independence and simplified security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent scan and this finding.

params.name: scanId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent vulnerability scan.

params.name: findingId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific vulnerability finding.

definition.description: Stores records of OSINT investigation tasks initiated by a specific user. The 'userId' field within each document ensures authorization independence by matching the path wildcard.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who initiated this OSINT task.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific OSINT task.

definition.description: Stores individual OSINT results found within a user's task. CRITICAL: This document must include a 'userId' field, denormalized from the owning user, for authorization independence and simplified security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent OSINT task and this result.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent OSINT task.

params.name: resultId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific OSINT result.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence and Query as Authorization Predicates (QAPs) to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability. All user-specific data is nested under a top-level `/users/{userId}` collection. This immediately scopes all data to its owner, simplifying security rules and enabling efficient user-based queries.

For entities like 'Operation', 'Report', 'KnowledgeItem', 'VulnerabilityScan', and 'OsintTask', the structure `/users/{userId}/<collectionName>/{documentId}` is used. Each document within these collections is expected to explicitly contain a `userId` field that matches the `{userId}` wildcard in the path. This denormalization is crucial for Authorization Independence, as it allows security rules to evaluate access (`request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` or `request.auth.uid == {userId}`) without performing expensive and non-atomic `get()` operations on parent documents. This directly supports QAPs, as a `list` operation on `/users/{current_uid}/operations` automatically filters for the authenticated user's operations, making the query itself the authorization predicate.

For nested user-owned data, specifically 'VulnerabilityFinding' and 'OsintResult', the hierarchical structure is continued: `/users/{userId}/vulnerabilityScans/{scanId}/findings/{findingId}` and `/users/{userId}/osintTasks/{taskId}/results/{resultId}`. **Critically, for Authorization Independence, documents in these subcollections ('findings' and 'results') MUST also include the denormalized `userId` field.** This field, copied from their parent 'VulnerabilityScan' or 'OsintTask' (which also contain `userId`), enables atomic authorization checks directly on the subcollection document without needing to `get()` the parent or root user document. This ensures that even deeply nested data remains easily securable with simple `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` rules and enables atomic batch/transactional writes involving multiple levels of the hierarchy. QAPs are supported here too, as listing findings for a given scan implicitly requires authorization for the `{userId}` path segment.

Global, platform-managed entities such as 'AIModelConfiguration' and 'ToolDefinition', which are not tied to a specific user, are placed in top-level collections like `/aiModelConfigurations/{modelConfigId}`. These collections would typically have read-only access for all authenticated users, or be restricted to administrative roles defined in a separate DBAC (Database Access Control) collection (e.g., `/roles_admin/{uid}`). This structural segregation ensures that data with different security postures do not mix, simplifying global access rules. QAPs apply here by allowing all authenticated users to list available tools or AI configurations, with write access being restricted through explicit role checks (e.g., `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))`).

Overall, this design eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules, promotes consistent access patterns, and ensures that the system is inherently secure, scalable, and easy to debug.
